Basically, Numbering is the concept to process assigning unique values to the key fields of business entities during Create and Create By Association Operations.

Unmanaged Early Numbering:

  • Developers need to implement custom code logic to create and assign keys values during the creation and create by association operation. 
  • %is_draft – It is a component of the transactional key (%tky) in RAP. 
    • It tells the framework whether to operate on the draft table or the active persistent table for a given operation. 
    • %is_draft = mk-on reads from draft table 
    • %is_draft = mk-off reads from active table 

Problem Statement

A sales clerk begins creating a Sales Order in Fiori. The order header is saved as a draft, but items need to be added immediately under that draft. To ensure consistency, the system must generate a unique Sales Order ID right away and assign incremental item numbers without waiting for activation.

Importance:

  • Early numbering guarantees unique identification across drafts and active records.

  • Associations ensure header–item linkage is preserved.

  • Draft handling allows users to save incomplete transactions safely and activate later without losing data integrity.

Behavior Definition of Root Entity

early numbering → enables key generation during root creation.

unmanaged implementation in class zbp_i_vd_head unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for ZI_VD_HEAD alias Header
draft table zdft_vd_head
lock master
total etag CreatedOn
authorization master ( instance )
early numbering // Early numbering declaration in root entity
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  association _item { create; }
  field ( readonly : update ) CustNo;

  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Resume;
  draft action Discard;
  draft determine action Prepare;
}
mapping for zvd_t_hsale {
  CustNo     = cust_no;
  SalesOrder = sales_order;
  CreatedOn  = created_on;
  EntryTime  = entry_time;
  Name       = name;
  DocCat     = doc_cat;
}


Behavior Definition of Item Entity

early numbering → enables key generation during item creation.

define behavior for ZI_VD_ITEM alias Item
draft table zdft_vd_item
lock dependent by _head
authorization dependent by _head
early numbering // Early numbering declaration in item entity
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly ) CustNo;
  association _head;

  mapping for zvd_t_isale {
    CustNo     = cust_no;
    SalesOrder = sales_order;
    DocItem    = doc_item;
    MatNumber  = mat_number;
    MatGroup   = mat_group;
    CreatedOn  = created_on;
  }
}

Behavior Pool Implementation

This method ensures that every new entity gets a unique identifier (CustNo) during creation if it wasn’t already provided.

UUID Generation:

  • cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ) generates a globally unique key.

  • This avoids collisions and guarantees uniqueness across drafts and active records.

Mapped Root:

  • %cid → correlates client-side temporary IDs with backend entities.

  • %key → holds the generated or existing key.

  • %is_draft → ensures draft state is preserved.

  • CustNo → the actual business key stored in the entity.

METHOD earlynumbering_create.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity) WHERE CustNo IS INITIAL.
    DATA(ls_uuid) = cl_system_uuid=>create_uuid_x16_static( ).

    APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = entity-%cid
                    %key      = VALUE #( CustNo = ls_uuid )
                    %is_draft = entity-%is_draft
                    CustNo    = ls_uuid ) TO mapped-header.
  ENDLOOP.

  LOOP AT entities INTO entity WHERE CustNo IS NOT INITIAL.
    APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = entity-%cid
                    %key      = entity-%key
                    %is_draft = entity-%is_draft ) TO mapped-header.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Mapped Item:

This method handles early numbering when creating items via association (CREATE-BY-ASSOCIATION).

  • First, it retrieves the maximum cust_no from the database table zvd_t_isale for the current entity.

  • Then, it checks existing items in the helper class buffer (gt_isale) to ensure numbering continuity.

  • For each new item (DocItem IS INITIAL), it increments the maximum by 10 and assigns the new number.

  • %cid → client-side correlation ID.

  • %key → generated key with CustNo.

  • %is_draft → preserves draft state.

  • CustNo → ensures linkage to the root entity.

METHOD earlynumbering_cba_item.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
    SELECT MAX( cust_no ) FROM zvd_t_isale
      INTO (lv_max)
      WHERE cust_no = -CustNo.

    LOOP AT zcl_vd_helper=>get_instance( )->gt_isale INTO DATA(item)
      WHERE cust_no = entity-%key-CustNo.
      IF item-doc_item > lv_max.
        lv_max = item-doc_item.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    LOOP AT entity-%target INTO DATA(t_item) WHERE DocItem IS INITIAL.
      lv_max += 10.

      APPEND VALUE #( %cid      = t_item-%cid
                      %key      = VALUE #( CustNo = entity-%key-CustNo )
                      %is_draft = t_item-%is_draft
                      CustNo    = entity-%key-CustNo ) TO mapped-item.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

  • Item inherits lock and authorization from the header.

  • Early numbering ensures item keys are generated consistently.

  • Association links items back to the header BO.

Persistence Methods

  • Create Method
METHOD create.
  LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
    DATA(header) = CORRESPONDING zvd_t_hsale( entity MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

    header-created_on = sy-datum.
    header-entry_time = sy-datum.
    header-name       = sy-uname.

    APPEND header TO gt_hsale.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.
  • Create-By-Association Method
METHOD cba.
  LOOP AT entities_cba INTO DATA(entity_cba).
    LOOP AT entity_cba-%target INTO DATA(target_item).
      DATA(item) = CORRESPONDING zvd_t_isale( target_item MAPPING FROM ENTITY ).

      item-cust_no   = entity_cba-%key-CustNo.
      item-created_on = sy-datum.

      APPEND item TO gt_isale.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMETHOD.

Result

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

By combining Draft Handling, Associations, and Early Numbering, RAP unmanaged scenarios can support complex business objects like Sales Orders, Purchase Orders. This approach ensures flexibility, control, and consistency in transactional applications.

Thank You...

entry.title: Link Handlers cannot be registered as the application is signed - Eclipse MAC
entry.content:
What Are Link Handlers?

When you click an adt:// link somewhere — SAP GUI, a wiki, a colleague's message — macOS needs to figure out which app should open it. That mapping is handled by link handlers.

In Eclipse, you find them under Preferences → General → Link Handlers. Two entries matter for ABAP developers:

  • adt – How adt:// links open ABAP objects (classes, programs, etc.) in your Eclipse workspace.
  • eclipse+command – Lets URLs trigger Eclipse commands, like opening a specific view.

Both should show a checked box and "This application" in the Handler column. If you only have one Eclipse on your Mac, you might never notice a problem. But with two installations (say one for ABAP, one for Java), wrong link routing gets annoying fast.

The Problem

You open Link Handlers and see this at the top:

⛔Link Handlers cannot be registered as the application is signed.

Checkboxes grayed out. Handler column says "Other application." Sometimes you also get this when clicking around the page:

Cannot invoke "org.eclipse.urischeme.ISchemeInformation.schemeIsHandledByOther()"
because "schemeInfo.information" is null

You click an adt:// link and either the wrong Eclipse opens, or nothing happens.

Why This Happens on macOS

macOS maps URL schemes to apps using the Info.plist inside each app bundle (/Applications/Eclipse.app/Contents/Info.plist). A key called CFBundleURLTypes lists which schemes the app claims. macOS reads it and stores the mapping in its Launch Services database. On top of that, plugins like ADT can also register schemes at runtime.

Here's where it breaks. Eclipse ships code-signed. The signature covers the entire bundle, including Info.plist. When Eclipse tries to modify the plist at runtime to register a scheme, macOS blocks it because that would invalidate the signature. The Link Handlers page sees the restriction and grays everything out.

The Fix

Two parts to this: first, make sure your Info.plist has the right URL scheme entries, then fix the code signature that's blocking everything.

⚠️Close Eclipse before you start.

Step 1: Check your Info.plist for CFBundleURLTypes

Open the plist:

sudo nano /Applications/Eclipse.app/Contents/Info.plist

Look for a CFBundleURLTypes key. The default Eclipse plist usually already has eclipse+command and eclipse+mpc, but not adt. You need all three in a single CFBundleURLTypes array. If the key already exists, add the adt entry into the existing array. If it's missing entirely, add this block just before the closing </dict> at the end of the file:

<key>CFBundleURLTypes</key>
<array>
    <dict>
        <key>CFBundleURLName</key>
        <string>Eclipse Command</string>
        <key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
        <array>
            <string>eclipse+command</string>
        </array>
    </dict>
    <dict>
        <key>CFBundleURLName</key>
        <string>Eclipse Marketplace</string>
        <key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
        <array>
            <string>eclipse+mpc</string>
        </array>
    </dict>
    <dict>
        <key>CFBundleURLName</key>
        <string>ADT Link Handler</string>
        <key>CFBundleURLSchemes</key>
        <array>
            <string>adt</string>
        </array>
    </dict>
</array>

Save and exit (Ctrl+O, then Ctrl+X).

Important: Make sure there's only one CFBundleURLTypes key in the entire file. If you're adding the adt entry to an existing array, don't create a second CFBundleURLTypes block — duplicate keys in a plist don't merge. macOS only reads one of them, and you'll get unpredictable results. I made this mistake editing in nano and it caused the schemeInfo.information is null error.

Step 2: Remove the existing code signature

sudo codesign --remove-signature /Applications/Eclipse.app

Don't try to open Eclipse after this. macOS won't launch an unsigned app. Move straight to Step 3.

Step 3: Re-sign with an ad-hoc signature

sudo codesign --force --deep --sign - /Applications/Eclipse.app

The - means ad-hoc — no developer certificate needed. --deep signs all the nested binaries inside the bundle too.

Step 4: Re-register with Launch Services

/System/Library/Frameworks/CoreServices.framework/Versions/A/Frameworks/LaunchServices.framework/Versions/A/Support/lsregister -R -f /Applications/Eclipse.app

Tells macOS to re-read the app bundle and update its URL scheme mappings.

Step 5: Open Eclipse and check

Go to Preferences → General → Link Handlers. Both adt and eclipse+command should now show checked and marked "This application".

All terminal commands together

# Close Eclipse first, then:
sudo codesign --remove-signature /Applications/Eclipse.app
sudo codesign --force --deep --sign - /Applications/Eclipse.app
/System/Library/Frameworks/CoreServices.framework/Versions/A/Frameworks/LaunchServices.framework/Versions/A/Support/lsregister -R -f /Applications/Eclipse.app
# Open Eclipse → Preferences → Link Handlers

What I Tried That Didn't Work

Sharing these so you don't waste time on the same dead ends.

Re-signing without removing first. Tried just running codesign --force --deep --sign - on its own. Didn't help. The old signature's restrictions stuck around somehow. You have to remove first, then sign fresh.

Duplicate CFBundleURLTypes keys in the plist. This one cost me the most time. When editing in nano, I accidentally added a second CFBundleURLTypes block instead of adding entries into the existing one. Plist files don't merge duplicate keys — macOS only reads one of them. The result was the schemeInfo.information is null error, and eclipse+command stayed stuck on "Other application." If you see that error, open the plist and check for duplicate keys.

Running lsregister by itself. Doesn't do anything useful if the signature is still blocking registration. Necessary as a final step, but not enough on its own.

Environment

Tested on Eclipse 2025-03 (4.39) with ADT on macOS. Should work the same on any recent Eclipse version where you see the signed-application warning.

entry.title: Object Release State in Cloudification Repository Viewer for Clean Core Extensibility
entry.content:
Introduction

The updated Clean Core Extensibility whitepaper was introduced in August 2025 by SAP as an evolution of the 3-Tier Extensibility concept. This updated the 3-Tier Extensibility to a more detailed assessment of classic ABAP extensions based on the clean core level concept, allowing you as SAP customers and partners stepwise transformation to clean core at your own pace .

While using external APIs in your ABAP custom code, one of the first things to check as per the updated level concept is the release state of that API in the Cloudification Repository Viewer. The release state tells you how stable the object is, what contractual guarantee SAP provides for it, and how your ATC checks will treat any usage of it. Getting this right is important because it directly affects your clean core compliance and your upgrade readiness.

SAP categorizes all objects in the Cloudification Repository Viewer into five distinct states. Each state carries a specific meaning for your custom code, and knowing the difference will help you make better decisions when selecting or replacing APIs.

The Five Release States in the Cloudification Repository Viewer

Here is what each state means in practice and what you should do when you encounter it.

Released

A Released API is one that SAP has formally published with a stability contract. Backward compatibility is guaranteed across upgrades, so you can build on these objects with confidence. Released APIs correspond to clean core Level A.

Level A objects are 100% Cloud compliant, 100% Clean core, can be achieved On-Stack, Side-by-Side or Hybrid.

If you are starting new development, Released APIs are your first choice. They represent the safest and most future-proof option available to you.

Deprecated

A Deprecated API was once Released but SAP is now phasing it out. It still functions today, but it has a planned end of life. SAP recommends that you do not start any new development on it.

If your existing code already uses a Deprecated object, that code is treated as clean core Level B and flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Make sure to identify the successor API in the Cloudification Repository or the SAP Business Accelerator Hub and plan your migration. The sooner you move away from a Deprecated API, the less remediation work you will face when the object reaches its end-of-life date.

Classic API

Classic APIs are well-known, time-tested objects such as BAPIs and RFC-enabled function modules that SAP product experts nominated before a formal release process existed. They do not carry a formal stability contract, but they are upgrade stable and widely used across the SAP ecosystem.

Classic APIs correspond to clean core Level B and are flagged as Priority 3 in ATC checks. Keep in mind that while these objects are stable in practice, they do not carry the same forward-compatibility guarantee as Released APIs. Use them when no Released alternative is available and check the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the availability of the Released APIs as successors to Classic APIs.

Not to be Released

An API marked as Not to be Released is one that SAP has explicitly decided is not intended for external use. Any exemption for using such an object must include a migration plan toward a Released API.

These objects correspond to clean core Level C depending on the context and are flagged as Priority 2 in ATC checks. If you encounter this state, treat it as a signal to find a supported alternative as quickly as possible.

No API

An object classified as No API has no API contract whatsoever. Objects classified as noAPI must not be used, creates maximum technical debt.

This state is flagged as Priority 1 in ATC checks and must be addressed at the highest priority. If you find a noAPI object in your custom code, replacing it with a Released API must be your immediate next step.

Why Some APIs Are Listed as Both Released and Classic or Not to be Released and Classic

You may come across a scenario in the Cloudification Repository Viewer where the same API appears under both the Classic API and Released API categories or Not to be Released and Classic. This is a valid situation and worth understanding, because it can cause some confusion at first glance.

 

Take the CDS view I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS01 release, it appears as both Classic API and Released API.

 

 

What this tells you is that I_EARMARKEDFUNDSDOCUMENTITEM was formally Released in the 2025 FPS0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Released API. In this case, the clean core status will be Level A as Released API will take the precedence and ATC check will not give any message.

Similarly, take the Class CL_HTTP_UTILITY as an example. If you check this object in the Cloudification Repository Viewer for the 2022 SP0 release of SAP Cloud ERP Private, it is listed only as a Classic API. When you switch to the 2025 FPS0 release, it appears as both Classic API and Not to be Released, with a successor.

Both statuses are equally valid for this object. It carries the historical stability of a Classic API and now also has a formal stability contract as a Not to be Released, with a successor.

In this case, the clean core status will be Level B as Classic API will take the precedence and ATC check will give a Priority 3 message. You are recommended to move to the successor API in this case.

This is why it is recommended to check the Cloudification Repository Viewer against the specific release you are working with.

Conclusion

The Cloudification Repository Viewer continues to grow with each release as SAP adds more objects and updates their states. Use it as your starting point before selecting any API in your custom code. Checking the release state upfront will save you significant remediation work during upgrade cycles and help you stay clean core compliant from day one.

We will continue to share updates on clean core extensibility as the tooling and API landscape evolves. In the meantime, refer to the Cloudification Repository Viewer and the SAP Business Accelerator Hub for the latest state of the objects relevant to your development.

entry.title: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Congratulations to everyone who came along with us till the 4th week of the ABAP Developer Challenge. So far we have seen :

  • How to create a OData UI Service from Scratch using Joule Chat
  • How to use Joule - Explain feature to understand a block of code.
  • How to use Joule - RAP Predict Business Logic to create logic for specific tasks.

Let us wrap up with another interesting feature of Joule's ABAP AI Capability - The Joule Predictive Code Completion.

Quick Links

Please use this separate thread to ask your questions and discuss issues. 

Sample Scenario

We had asked you to create a field of type 'Date' during the RAP application creation.

Now let us use Determination function to calculate the number of years since the value in Date field. You can implement it while creating a new record using the app.

Task1: Create a determination and implement the corresponding method.

  1. Define a determination in the BDEF of the CDS view.Define Determination
  2. Now you can notice a small icon to the left of the determination definition. Click on it to create the implementation method for adding determination logic in the local handler class of the behavior pool of your entity. Save and activate the changes.Implement DeterminationFor additional information on Determinations and its syntax and use you can refer the BDEF ‘/DMO/R_Travel_D’. Alternatively you can also refer https://github.com/SAP-samples/abap-platform-rap100/blob/main/exercises/ex04

Task2: Enhance determination with Joule Code Prediction Capability

Use Joule's Predictive Code Completion feature to complete your code by providing simple prompts for each step in the logic.

Here please note that we should use the Toggle option to disable / enable Joule Code Prediction Capability by pressing  button in the toolbar.

Hint: Disable the Joule Code Prediction Capability if you want to give prompts for new steps / once finished to stop Automatic Code Prediction.

Note:- The logic and examples are mentioned just for your reference and to make the purpose of the challenge easy to understand. Please feel free to use your own logic and dataset.

To know how to use Predictive Code Completion follow this tutorial

Please note that the challenge will remain open for another week for anyone who is running late. 

Before we close for this year we would like to thank everyone who participated. We hope all of you enjoyed the challenge. Let us continue to explore how SAP Joule for developers, ABAP AI capabilities can support us with our day-to-day tasks when working with ABAP code, to increase our efficiency and to lower total cost of development.

Happy Jouling!!!

entry.title: KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:  A Key Performance Indicator (KPI) is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its key objectives. In SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs are used to display important business metrics in a clear and visual format. They help users quickly monitor performance, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.

KPI (Key Performance Indicator):  It is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its objectives. KPIs help businesses monitor performance, identify trends, and support data-driven decision-making.

In modern enterprise applications built with SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs provide a quick overview of key business metrics directly within dashboards and analytical applications.

KPI in Analytical List Page:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a powerful Fiori Elements template that combines:

  • KPIs
  • Charts
  • Filters
  • Tables

KPIs are displayed at the Top of the page and help users quickly analyze business data.

Note: In this article, I have not covered the creation of the CDS View, OData service exposure, or the application creation in SAP BTP, as these topics were already explained in my previous blogs. In this post, I focus only on providing an example of the KPI annotations and explaining where these annotations are added in the project.

CDS:

You can add the KPI annotations in the Metadata Extension file. This approach helps keep UI-related annotations separate from the core CDS view and allows developers to manage UI configurations more efficiently.

KPI Annotation:

.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]

Once the annotations are added in the Metadata Extension, you can configure the KPI annotation in the manifest.json file so that it is recognized and displayed in the Analytical List Page application.

qualifier: 'KPIAMT'
 

"keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }

Path:

sap.ui.generic.app → pages → AnalyticalListPage → component → settings → keyPerformanceIndicators
 "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },

Note : After that, ensure that the model used for the KPI configuration is also created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name defined for the KPI must be maintained in the models configuration so that the application can correctly retrieve the KPI data from the OData service.

The model name used in the KPI configuration (for example, KPI1) must also be created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name should be maintained .

Path:

sap.ui5 → models → KPI1

    "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
"models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },

Once all the configurations are completed, the KPI is successfully created. You can see the KPI displayed in the Analytical List Page as shown in the screenshot below for your reference.

Full Codes: This code example covers the Analytical List Page, including the configuration of Visual Filters and KPI annotations used in the application.

1.CDS View:
-------------
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
___________________________________
2.Meta data Extension:
------------------------
@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]


annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.title: 'Total Amount'
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}
__________________________________
3. Annotation.xml file:
-----------------------------
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
           
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>
 _____________________________          
4. Manifest.json File:
----------------------------------------------
{
    "_version": "1.82.0",
    "sap.app": {
        "id": "analyticalreport",
        "type": "application",
        "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
        "applicationVersion": {
            "version": "0.0.1"
        },
        "title": "{{appTitle}}",
        "description": "{{appDescription}}",
        "resources": "resources.json",
        "sourceTemplate": {
            "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:alp",
            "version": "1.20.4",
            "toolsId": "530787e5-fdb9-4d9d-87bf-ca33db19cf1d"
        },
        "dataSources": {
            "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL',Version='0001')/$value/",
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL.xml"
                }
            },
            "annotation": {
                "type": "ODataAnnotation",
                "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
                "settings": {
                    "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
                }
            },
            "mainService": {
                "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/",
                "type": "OData",
                "settings": {
                    "annotations": [
                        "ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_ANNO_MDL",
                        "annotation"
                    ],
                    "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
                    "odataVersion": "2.0"
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.ui": {
        "technology": "UI5",
        "icons": {
            "icon": "",
            "favIcon": "",
            "phone": "",
            "phone@2": "",
            "tablet": "",
            "tablet@2": ""
        },
        "deviceTypes": {
            "desktop": true,
            "tablet": true,
            "phone": true
        }
    },
    "sap.ui5": {
        "flexEnabled": true,
        "dependencies": {
            "minUI5Version": "1.145.0",
            "libs": {
                "sap.m": {},
                "sap.ui.core": {},
                "sap.ushell": {},
                "sap.f": {},
                "sap.ui.comp": {},
                "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
                "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
            }
        },
        "contentDensities": {
            "compact": true,
            "cozy": true
        },
        "models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
            "@i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
            }
        },
        "resources": {
            "css": []
        },
        "routing": {
            "config": {},
            "routes": [],
            "targets": {}
        }
    },
    "sap.ui.generic.app": {
        "_version": "1.3.0",
        "settings": {
            "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
            "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
            "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
            "showDraftToggle": false
        },
        "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },
                "pages": {
                    "ObjectPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                        "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                        "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
                        "component": {
                            "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    },
    "sap.fiori": {
        "registrationIds": [],
        "archeType": "analytical"
    }
}

 

Conclusion : In conclusion, KPIs in SAP Fiori Elements provide a quick and effective way to monitor critical business metrics. By presenting key values in a visual and easy-to-understand format, KPIs help users track performance and take timely actions to improve business outcomes.

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: RAP: Factory Action to copy Billing Header and its Item(s) using Create by association
entry.content:
In this blog, I am going to explain how we can use create by association with factory action in SAP RAP environment for complex use case like copying duplicate billing header with its associated child entities.

Let's understand what action in RAP is, and what is factory action first.

If you want to perform any operation other than those four basic operations (CRUD), we can use action keyword in RAP. It helps us to do custom action like approve billing doc or change any field value like status.

We have three types of actions in RAP:

  1. Instance Action: we need to select a record to perform action.
  2. Static Actions: that are not tied to a specific instance.
  3. Factory Actions: used for creating the copy of already existing instance. We will see this more in details.

Create by Association: In scenario where we cannot create child entity as it is depend on header, we need to use create by association. Used to create deep entity.

So, let's start with the steps to generate our basic application. Please have a look.

1. My header table look like this: 👇

@EndUserText.label : 'Billing Header'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table ztb_billheader {

  key client         : abap.clnt not null;
  key bill_id        : abap.numc(10) not null;
  bill_type          : abap.char(4);
  bill_date          : abap.dats;
  customer_id        : kunnr;
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'ztb_billheader.currency'
  net_amount         : abap.curr(15,2);
  currency           : waers;
  sales_org          : vkorg;
  createdby          : syuname;
  createdat          : timestamp;
  lastchangedby      : syuname;
  lastchangedat      : timestamp;
  locallastchangedat : timestamp;
  status             : abap.char(1);
  statustext         : abap.char(10);

}

I am not using managed numbering here as I want custom Bill Id.

2. My Item table look like this: 👇

@EndUserText.label : 'Billing Item'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table ztb_billitem {

  key client         : abap.clnt not null;
  key bill_id        : abap.char(10) not null;
  key item_no        : abap.numc(6) not null;
  material_id        : matnr;
  description        : abap.char(40);
  @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'ztb_billitem.uom'
  quantity           : abap.quan(13,3);
  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode : 'ztb_billitem.currency'
  item_amount        : abap.curr(15,2);
  currency           : waers;
  uom                : meins;
  createdby          : syuname;
  createdat          : timestamp;
  lastchangedby      : syuname;
  lastchangedat      : timestamp;
  locallastchangedat : timestamp;

}

3. I have created interface view on top of header table: You can skip that also. It looks like this: 👇

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface View for Bill header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define view entity ZI_BillHeader as select from ztb_billheader
{
    key bill_id as BillId,
    bill_type as BillType,
    bill_date as BillDate,
    customer_id as CustomerId,
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency'
    net_amount as NetAmount,
    currency as Currency,
    sales_org as SalesOrg,
    createdby as Createdby,
    createdat as Createdat,
    lastchangedby as Lastchangedby,
    lastchangedat as Lastchangedat,
    locallastchangedat as Locallastchangedat,
    status as status,
    statustext as StatusText
}

4. I have created root view on top of header interface view: 👇

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Root View Bill Header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity ZR_BillHeader as select from ZI_BillHeader
composition[0..*] of ZI_BillItem as _Item
{
    key BillId,
    BillType,
    BillDate,
    CustomerId,
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency'
    NetAmount,
    Currency,
    SalesOrg,
    Createdby,
    Createdat,
    Lastchangedby,
    Lastchangedat,
    Locallastchangedat,
    status,
    StatusText,
    _Item // Make association public
}

5. I have created interface view on top of item table: 👇

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface View for Bill Item'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

define view entity ZI_BillItem 
as select from ztb_billitem
association to parent ZR_BillHeader as _header on $projection.BillId = _header.BillId
{
    key bill_id as BillId,
    key item_no as ItemNo,
    material_id as MaterialId,
    description as Description,
    @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Uom'
    quantity as Quantity,
    @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency'
    item_amount as ItemAmount,
    currency as Currency,
    uom as Uom,
    createdby as Createdby,
    createdat as Createdat,
    lastchangedby as Lastchangedby,
    lastchangedat as Lastchangedat,
    locallastchangedat as Locallastchangedat,
    _header
}

6. Created projection view on top of root view: 👇

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection View Bill Header'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define root view entity ZP_BillHeader
  provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on ZR_BillHeader
{
  key BillId,
      BillType,
      BillDate,
      CustomerId,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency'
      NetAmount,
      Currency,
      SalesOrg,
      Createdby,
      Createdat,
      Lastchangedby,
      Lastchangedat,
      Locallastchangedat,
      @ObjectModel.text.element: ['StatusText']
      status,
      StatusText,
      /* Associations */
      _Item : redirected to composition child ZP_BillItem
}

7. Similarly created projection view for item entity: 👇

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Projection View Bill Item'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view entity ZP_BillItem
  as projection on ZI_BillItem
{
  key BillId,
  key ItemNo,
      MaterialId,
      Description,
      @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'Uom'
      Quantity,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'Currency'
      ItemAmount,
      Currency,
      Uom,
      Createdby,
      Createdat,
      Lastchangedby,
      Lastchangedat,
      Locallastchangedat,
      /* Associations */
      _header : redirected to parent ZP_BillHeader
}

8. My metadata looks like this: 👇 (Please make sure I haven't given labels for each field, so in UI for few field it will missing. My core agenda is to elaborate factory action) 

Header: 

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
@UI.headerInfo:{
typeName: 'Billing Document',
typeNamePlural: 'Billing Documents',
title: { type: #STANDARD, label: 'Biiling Document', value: 'BillId' }}
annotate entity ZP_BillHeader with
{
  .facet: [{ //id: 'BillDocHeader',
  label: 'Billing header',
  position: 10,
  purpose: #STANDARD,
  type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE
   },
   { //id: 'BillDocItem',
   label: 'Bill Item',
   position: 20,
   purpose: #STANDARD,
   type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
   targetElement: '_Item'
   }]

  .lineItem: [{ position:10 },
   { position: 10,
   type: #FOR_ACTION,
  dataAction: 'ApproveBill',
  label: 'Approve Bill' },
     { position: 20,
   type: #FOR_ACTION,
  dataAction: 'CopyBilldoc',
  label: 'Copy Bill' }]
  .selectionField: [{position: 1  }]
  .identification: [{ position: 1  }]
  BillId;
  .lineItem: [{ position:20 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 2  }]
  BillType;
  .lineItem: [{ position:30 }]
  BillDate;
  .lineItem: [{ position:40 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 3  }]
  CustomerId;
  .lineItem: [{ position:50 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 4  }]
  NetAmount;
  .lineItem: [{ position:60 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 5 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position:70 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 6  }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position:80 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 7  }]
  .textArrangement: #TEXT_ONLY
  status;

}

Item: 

@Metadata.layer: #CORE
annotate entity ZP_BillItem with
{
  .facet: [ {
    id:       'BillDocItem',
    purpose:  #STANDARD,
    type:     #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
    label:    'Billing Doc Item',
    position: 10
  }]
  .lineItem: [{ position:10 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 1 }]
  BillId;
  .lineItem: [{ position:20 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 1 }]
  ItemNo;
  .lineItem: [{ position:30 }]
  .identification: [{ position: 1 }]
  MaterialId;
  .lineItem: [{ position:40 }]
  Description;
  .lineItem: [{ position:50 }]
  Quantity;
  .lineItem: [{ position:70 }]
  ItemAmount;
  .lineItem: [{ position:80 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position:60 }]
  Uom;
  .lineItem: [{ position:90 }]
  Createdby;
  .lineItem: [{ position:100 }]
  Createdat;
  .lineItem: [{ position:110 }]

  /* Associations */
  _header;

}

Now we have basic structure ready !!! Main steps for our topic start from here.

9. Create behavior definition on top of root entity. Just right click on the root view entity and select new behavior definition as shown below 👇

Once behavior definition is generated, use quick fix to implement behavior class. 

10. Now add below syntax in behavior definition:

  factory action CopyBilldoc [1];

The whole behavior definition looks like this: 

managed implementation in class zbp_r_billheader unique;
strict ( 2 );

with draft;
define behavior for ZR_BillHeader //alias <alias_name>
persistent table ztb_billheader
draft table ztb_billhdr_drf
lock master total etag Lastchangedat
authorization master ( instance, global )
etag master Locallastchangedat
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Resume;
  draft determine action Prepare;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  field ( readonly : update, mandatory : create ) BillId;


  action ( features : instance ) ApproveBill result [1] $self;
  factory action CopyBilldoc [1];
  side effects
  {
    action CopyBilldoc affects $self;
  }
  association _Item { create; with draft; }

  mapping for ztb_billheader
    {
      BillId             = bill_id;
      BillType           = bill_type;
      BillDate           = bill_date;
      CustomerId         = customer_id;
      NetAmount          = net_amount;
      Currency           = currency;
      SalesOrg           = sales_org;
      CreatedBy          = createdby;
      CreateDat          = createdat;
      LastChangedBy      = lastchangedby;
      LastChangeDat      = lastchangedat;
      LocalLastChangeDat = locallastchangedat;
      status             = status;
      StatusText         = statustext;
    }
}

define behavior for ZI_BillItem //alias <alias_name>
persistent table ztb_billitem
draft table ztb_billitem_drf
lock dependent by _header
authorization dependent by _header
//etag master <field_name>
{
  update;
  delete;
  field ( readonly : update, mandatory : create ) BillId, ItemNo;
  association _header { with draft; }

  mapping for ztb_billitem
    {
      BillId             = bill_id;
      ItemNo             = item_no;
      MaterialId         = material_id;
      Description        = description;
      Quantity           = quantity;
      ItemAmount         = item_amount;
      Currency           = currency;
      Uom                = uom;
      CreatedBy          = createdby;
      CreateDat          = createdat;
      LastChangedBy      = lastchangedby;
      LastChangeDat      = lastchangedat;
      LocalLastChangeDat = locallastchangedat;
    }
}

Here, I have added extra things like draft, custom action and side effects. But for now, just focus on the line number 24.

11. Activate and use quick fix to generate this method in behavior class. 

12. Use below code inside the generated factory action method. 👇

METHOD CopyBilldoc.

    " Declaration of internal tables.

    DATA: lt_bill     TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZR_BillHeader\\ZR_BillHeader,
          lt_billitem TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZR_BillHeader\_item.

    DATA: lv_billId TYPE c LENGTH 10.
   " Reading highest existing bill id and incrementing it by 1 to use as new bill id
    SELECT BillId FROM ZR_BillHeader ORDER BY BillId DESCENDING
        INTO _billid UP TO 1 ROWS.
    ENDSELECT.

    lv_billId = lv_billId + 1.

   " Read by association 

    READ ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader  IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_data1)
    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader BY \_item
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_data2)
    FAILED failed.
   
   " Appending to create a new structure
    LOOP AT lt_data1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_data1>).

      APPEND VALUE #( %cid = keys[ KEY entity %key = <ls_data1>-%key ]-%cid

                      "%is_draft = keys[ KEY entity %key = <ls_data1>-%key ]-%param-%is_draft

                      %data = CORRESPONDING #( <ls_data1> EXCEPT BillId )

                      ) TO lt_bill ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<new_bill_doc>).

      <new_bill_doc>-BillId = CONV #( lv_billId ).
      <new_bill_doc>-BillDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date(  ).

      LOOP AT lt_data2 INTO DATA(ls_data2).
        APPEND VALUE #(
        %cid_ref = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
        "BillId = <new_bill_doc>-BillId
        %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
        BillId = <new_bill_doc>-BillId
        MaterialId = ls_data2-MaterialId
        ItemNo = ls_data2-ItemNo ) )
         ) TO lt_billitem ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<new_bill_item>).

      ENDLOOP.

    ENDLOOP.

    "Create BO instance by copy.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
    CREATE FIELDS ( BillId BillDate BillType Currency CustomerId NetAmount SalesOrg )
    WITH VALUE #( (
    %cid = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
    BillId = <new_bill_doc>-BillId
    BillDate = <new_bill_doc>-BillDate
    Currency = <new_bill_doc>-Currency
    CustomerId = <new_bill_doc>-CustomerId
    NetAmount = <new_bill_doc>-NetAmount
    SalesOrg = <new_bill_doc>-SalesOrg ) )
    CREATE BY \_item FIELDS ( ItemNo MaterialId )
    WITH VALUE #( (
    %cid_ref = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
    %target = VALUE #( FOR i IN lt_billitem
        FOR j IN i-%target (
    %cid = j-ItemNo
    ItemNo = j-ItemNo
    MaterialId = j-MaterialId
) ) ) )
    MAPPED DATA(mapped_create).

    " Set values to front end.

    mapped-ZR_BillHeader = mapped_create-ZR_BillHeader.


  ENDMETHOD.

The whole my behavior class looks like this: 👇

CLASS lhc_ZR_BillHeader DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.

    METHODS get_instance_authorizations FOR INSTANCE AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR ZR_BillHeader RESULT result.

    METHODS get_global_authorizations FOR GLOBAL AUTHORIZATION
      IMPORTING REQUEST requested_authorizations FOR ZR_BillHeader RESULT result.
    METHODS ApproveBill FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION ZR_BillHeader~ApproveBill RESULT result.
    METHODS get_instance_features FOR INSTANCE FEATURES
      IMPORTING keys REQUEST requested_features FOR ZR_BillHeader RESULT result.
    METHODS copybilldoc FOR MODIFY
      IMPORTING keys FOR ACTION zr_billheader~copybilldoc.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS lhc_ZR_BillHeader IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD get_instance_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_global_authorizations.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD ApproveBill.

*    DATA: lt_data TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE ZR_BillHeader.
*    DATA: lt_result TYPE TABLE FOR READ RESULT ZR_BillHeader.
*
*    " Read current data
*    READ ENTITIES OF zr_billheader IN LOCAL MODE
*      ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
*      ALL FIELDS
*      WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
*      RESULT DATA(lt_billheader).
*
*    LOOP AT lt_billheader INTO DATA(ls_order).
*      APPEND VALUE #(
*      %tky = ls_order-%tky
*      status = 'A' ) TO lt_data.
*    ENDLOOP.
*    " Prepare update
*    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
*      ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
*      UPDATE FIELDS ( status )
*      WITH lt_data.
*
*
*    READ ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
*    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
*    ALL FIELDS WITH
*    CORRESPONDING #( keys )
*    RESULT lt_result.
*
*    result = VALUE #(
*    FOR ls IN lt_result
*    ( %tky = ls-%tky
*    %param = ls ) ).

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
               ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
                  UPDATE FROM VALUE #( FOR key IN keys
                  ( %tky = key-%tky
                    status = 'A' " Approved
                    StatusText = 'Approved'
                    %control-status = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
                    %control-StatusText = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) )
                  FAILED failed
                  REPORTED reported.

    "Read changed data for action result
    READ ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
      ALL FIELDS WITH
      CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(billheaders).

    result = VALUE #( FOR billheader IN billheaders
             ( %tky   = billheader-%tky
               %param = billheader ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get_instance_features.
    READ ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
  ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
     FIELDS (  BillId status )
     WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
   RESULT DATA(BillHeaders)
   FAILED failed.

    result =
      VALUE #( FOR BillHeader IN BillHeaders
        ( %key = BillHeader-%key
          %features-%action-ApproveBill = COND #( WHEN BillHeader-status = 'A'
                                                        THEN if_abap_behv=>fc-o-disabled
                                                        ELSE if_abap_behv=>fc-o-enabled )
         ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD CopyBilldoc.

    " Copy records assigned to internal table.

    DATA: lt_bill     TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZR_BillHeader\\ZR_BillHeader,
          lt_billitem TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE ZR_BillHeader\_item.

    DATA: lv_billId TYPE c LENGTH 10.

    SELECT BillId FROM ZR_BillHeader ORDER BY BillId DESCENDING
        INTO _billid UP TO 1 ROWS.
    ENDSELECT.

    lv_billId = lv_billId + 1.


    READ ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader  IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_data1)
    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader BY \_item
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_data2)
    FAILED failed.

    LOOP AT lt_data1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_data1>).

      APPEND VALUE #( %cid = keys[ KEY entity %key = <ls_data1>-%key ]-%cid

                      "%is_draft = keys[ KEY entity %key = <ls_data1>-%key ]-%param-%is_draft

                      %data = CORRESPONDING #( <ls_data1> EXCEPT BillId )

                      ) TO lt_bill ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<new_bill_doc>).

      <new_bill_doc>-BillId = CONV #( lv_billId ).
      <new_bill_doc>-BillDate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date(  ).

      LOOP AT lt_data2 INTO DATA(ls_data2).
        APPEND VALUE #(
        %cid_ref = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
        "BillId = <new_bill_doc>-BillId
        %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
        BillId = <new_bill_doc>-BillId
        MaterialId = ls_data2-MaterialId
        ItemNo = ls_data2-ItemNo ) )
         ) TO lt_billitem ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<new_bill_item>).

      ENDLOOP.

    ENDLOOP.

    "Create BO instance by copy.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZR_BillHeader IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY ZR_BillHeader
    CREATE FIELDS ( BillId BillDate BillType Currency CustomerId NetAmount SalesOrg )
    WITH VALUE #( (
    %cid = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
    BillId = <new_bill_doc>-BillId
    BillDate = <new_bill_doc>-BillDate
    Currency = <new_bill_doc>-Currency
    CustomerId = <new_bill_doc>-CustomerId
    NetAmount = <new_bill_doc>-NetAmount
    SalesOrg = <new_bill_doc>-SalesOrg ) )
    CREATE BY \_item FIELDS ( ItemNo MaterialId )
    WITH VALUE #( (
    %cid_ref = <new_bill_doc>-%cid
    %target = VALUE #( FOR i IN lt_billitem
        FOR j IN i-%target (
    %cid = j-ItemNo
    ItemNo = j-ItemNo
    MaterialId = j-MaterialId
) ) ) )
    MAPPED DATA(mapped_create).

    " Set values to front end.

    mapped-ZR_BillHeader = mapped_create-ZR_BillHeader.


  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

13. Create behavior projection on top of root projection view. It will look like this 👇

projection;
strict ( 2 );

use draft;
define behavior for ZP_BillHeader //alias <alias_name>
{
  use create;
  use update;
  use delete;
  use action Edit;
  use action Activate;
  use action Discard;
  use action Resume;
  use action Prepare;
  use action ApproveBill;
  use action CopyBilldoc;
  use association _Item { create; with draft;}
}

define behavior for ZP_BillItem //alias <alias_name>
{
  use update;
  use delete;

  use association _header { with draft; }
}

14. Now create the service definition and expose both header and item projection: 

@EndUserText.label: 'Service Definition for Billing Doc'
define service ZSD_BillingDocument {
  expose ZP_BillHeader as BillingHeader;
  expose ZP_BillItem   as BillingItem;
}

15. On top of service definition, create a service binding. I have use Odata V4 as I am using draft functionality. 

16. Finally, it's a result time. Have a first look of our application. 👇

17. Now select any record and click on copyBill. I am selecting second record. 

18. Once I clicked on copyBill, We can see a new BillId is generated with same line items as of source record.

Conclusion: So, we have come to the end of our blog. We have seen how we can implement an use case related to factory action. I want to summarise important steps again required to implement factory action: 

  1. Declare factory action in behavior definition and activate it.
  2. Use quick fix to generate the method in implementation class. 
  3. Use Read by association to read both header and child entity.
  4. Append the new structure.
  5. Create new duplicate BO entry by using create by association.

Thank You for reading my blog. Do comment if you have any doubt or if you know any better way to do this. Good Bye 👋

entry.title: Overview Page in SAP Fiori Elements: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Fiori Elements provides several floorplans that help developers build enterprise applications quickly with minimal coding. One of these floorplans is the Overview Page (OVP). The Overview Page acts as a role-based dashboard that displays important business information using visual and interactive cards. It enables users to monitor key performance indicators, review important data, and take actions directly from a single screen.

Overview Page in Fiori Elements: The Overview Page (OVP) in SAP Fiori Elements is a role-based dashboard that displays important business information using cards.

Overview Page  is Dashboard of multiple cards showing business insights.

Note:  In this article, I have not covered the step-by-step process of creating the CDS View and OData Service. I have focused mainly on the key steps required to configure and implement the Overview Page in SAP Fiori Elements.

CDS View:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZOVR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'OverView Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true




define view Ztest_Overview_Report
  as select from Ztest_ALP_Report
{


      .selectionField: [{position: 10  }]
      @EndUserText.label: 'Month & Year'
  key Month_Year,
      @EndUserText.label: 'Total Amount'
      max(Amount) as Total_Amount


}

group by
  Month_Year

Consume CDS View in OData Service:

Overview Page (OVP) - BAS:

I have covered only the Analytical Card in the Overview Page. The remaining card types such as List Card, Table Card, KPI Card, and Link List Card are not included in this blog.

Please follow the steps below to create and configure the Analytical Card step by step in the SAP Fiori Elements Overview Page in BTP BAS.

 

 

Step1:

    <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_Overview_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Donut"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Sales Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Total_Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Total_Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>

Step2:

 <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="step2_PVQ">
                    <Record>
                        <PropertyValue Property="SortOrder">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.SortOrderType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Property" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Descending" Bool="true"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

Step3:

 

 <Annotation Term="UI.DataPoint" Qualifier="Step3_DPQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.DataPointType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="Month_Year"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Criticality" EnumMember="UI.CriticalityType/Information"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Total Amount"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Total Amount"/>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>

Step4:

<Annotation Term="UI.Identification" Qualifier="Step4_Q">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation">
                            <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="Total_Amount"/>
                            <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="Total Amount"/>
                            <Annotation Term="UI.Importance" EnumMember="UI.ImportanceType/Medium"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>

Step5:

 

"cards": {
        "Nayak333": {
            "model": "mainModel",
            "template": "sap.ovp.cards.charts.analytical",
            "settings": {
                "title": "Analytical Card",
                "entitySet": "Ztest_Overview_Report",
                "dataPointAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.DataPoint#Step3_DPQ",
                "valueSelectionInfo": "Total_Amount",
                "presentationAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.PresentationVariant#step2_PVQ",
                "chartAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.Chart#Step_CQ",
                "identificationAnnotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.Identification#Step4_Q"
            }
        }
    },

 

Full Annotation Code:

<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_Overview_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Donut"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Sales Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Total_Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Total_Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="step2_PVQ">
                    <Record>
                        <PropertyValue Property="SortOrder">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.SortOrderType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Property" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Descending" Bool="true"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.DataPoint" Qualifier="Step3_DPQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.DataPointType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Value" Path="Month_Year"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Criticality" EnumMember="UI.CriticalityType/Information"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Total Amount"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Total Amount"/>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.Identification" Qualifier="Step4_Q">
                    <Collection>
                        <Record Type="UI.DataFieldForIntentBasedNavigation">
                            <PropertyValue Property="SemanticObject" String="Total_Amount"/>
                            <PropertyValue Property="Label" String="Total Amount"/>
                            <Annotation Term="UI.Importance" EnumMember="UI.ImportanceType/Medium"/>
                        </Record>
                    </Collection>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>

 

 

Note:   Please follow the same steps to create the remaining card types in the Overview Page. By configuring them in a similar way, the other cards will also be displayed on the Overview Page.

 

Conclusion

The Overview Page in SAP Fiori Elements provides a powerful way to display business insights through a dashboard of visual cards. It helps users quickly monitor key metrics, identify important tasks, and navigate to detailed applications for further analysis. By combining data visualization and actionable insights, the Overview Page enables organizations to make faster and more informed decisions.

 

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak.
